If a Buddhist monk, a teacher, a Jain monk, a naked fakir, a
sooth-sayer, a trader of a town, and a fisherman be seen at the time,
the querist's thought would be about a thief, commander of army,
a merchant, a female servant, a soldier, a shop-keeper and a con-
demned criminal respectively.

When an ascetic is seen, the querist thinks about a person that
has gone abroad ; when a toddy-seller, about tending cattle ; and

Science of Limbs LI 439
when a hermit engaged in gleaning corn, about one that has come to
grief.
